title: Consultants, waivers, lack of bids relied on during incomplete overhaul of Syracuse I.T.
summary: A Syracuse I.T. payroll modernization project faced significant governance and procurement failures, including use of waivers to bypass competitive bidding, reliance on multiple consultants, and allegations that Ernst & Young used Advanced IT as a pass-through vendor. A whistleblower complaint and subsequent Common Council audit have exposed incomplete project oversight and documentation discrepancies by city leadership, with the Finance Commissioner admitting project delays impacted critical audit work.

political_signal.description: Common Council conducting formal audit of I.T. project governance; whistleblower complaint escalating vendor management and competitive bidding violations; Finance Commissioner acknowledging project mismanagement impact.

processing_notes: Article indicates incomplete project delivery and ongoing investigation; multiple consultant layers and waiver usage suggest procurement policy breakdown; financial amounts mentioned ($1.8M+) require audit verification; no resolution status indicated
